CONTENT.Club career: Muñoz played for Cerro from 2002 to 2008, scoring 4 goals.In 2009, he was transferred to Club Nacional de Football, making his debut in a 2-1 home win against Universidad San Martín de Porres in the 2009 Copa Libertadores on February 12, 2009. With Nacional he won the Uruguayan championship twice (2009 & 2011).In January 2012, he signed a new deal with the Paraguayan side Libertad.
CONTENT.External links: Rodrigo Muñoz at SoccerwayRodrigo Martin Muñoz at BDFA (in Spanish)
CONTENT.International career: On May 18, 2011 he was reserved to play a friendly match against Germany in Sinsheim. Muñoz was named in Uruguay's provisional squad for Copa América Centenario but was cut from the final squad.

SUMMARY: Rodrigo Martín Muñoz Salomón (Spanish pronunciation: [roˈðɾiɣo muˈɲos]; born 22 January 1982) is a Uruguayan former footballer and Paraguayan who played as a goalkeeper in the Paraguayan Primera División club Guarani.
